revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 春日神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 福岡県北九州市八幡西区藤田1-10-44 |位置 =　 |祭神 = 天児屋根命
武甕槌命
経津主命
黒田孝高命
黒田長政命他 |社格 = 県社
国史見在社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 春日宮
鳥野神社
黒田神社 |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |}} '''春日神社'''（春日神社）は[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[八幡西区]][[黒崎 (北九州市)|黒崎]]地区にある[[神社]]で、春日宮や黒田神社とも称される。 [[日本三代実録]]に記載される「筑前国鳥野神」に充てられる[[国史見在社]]の[[論社]]の一つ。毎年七月に春日宮へ奉納する[[黒崎祇園山笠]]は北九州の風物詩として有名。 ==祭神== 春日神（天児屋根命・武甕槌命・経津主命）の他、福岡藩祖である[[黒田如水]]、および初代藩主・[[黒田長政]]と[[黒田二十四騎]]を併せて祀る。 ==歴史== 古くは鳥野神社と称し、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観]]15年（[[873年]]）に神位の等級を進められた「鳥野神」に充てられている。中世は領主・[[麻生氏]]の崇敬篤く、近世に至り[[黒田氏]]が筑前へ入国すると黒崎宿・藤田の鎮守神として栄えた。後に藩祖・黒田如水および初代藩主・黒田長政を祀り、併せて黒田家家中で勲功のあった二十四人の武将を顕彰。江戸時代を通じて「黒田大明神」を称した。明治に至り春日宮を再称。[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）村社、[[昭和]]12年（[[1937年]]）、県社に列した。 ==祭事・年中行事== *祇園大祭（黒崎祇園山笠）：7月21日～23日 *秋季大祭（例大祭）：10月15日 ==所在地・交通== 福岡県北九州市八幡西区藤田1-10-44 *[[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[黒崎駅]]から徒歩10分 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:県社]] [[Category:八幡西区]]

{{神社 |名称 = 春日神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 福岡県北九州市八幡西区藤田1-10-44 |位置 =　 |祭神 = 天児屋根命
武甕槌命
経津主命
黒田孝高命
黒田長政命他 |社格 = 県社
国史見在社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 春日宮
鳥野神社
黒田神社 |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |}} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[八幡西区]][[黒崎 (北九州市)|黒崎]]地区にある[[神社]]で、春日宮や黒田神社とも称される。 [[日本三代実録]]に記載される「筑前国鳥野神」に充てられる[[国史見在社]]の[[論社]]の一つ。毎年七月に春日宮へ奉納する[[黒崎祇園山笠]]は北九州の風物詩として有名。 ==祭神== 春日神（天児屋根命・武甕槌命・経津主命）の他、福岡藩祖である[[黒田如水]]、および初代藩主・[[黒田長政]]と[[黒田二十四騎]]を併せて祀る。 ==歴史== 古くは鳥野神社と称し、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観]]15年（[[873年]]）に神位の等級を進められた「鳥野神」に充てられている。中世は領主・[[麻生氏]]の崇敬篤く、近世に至り[[黒田氏]]が筑前へ入国すると黒崎宿・藤田の鎮守神として栄えた。後に藩祖・黒田如水および初代藩主・黒田長政を祀り、併せて黒田家家中で勲功のあった二十四人の武将を顕彰。江戸時代を通じて「黒田大明神」を称した。明治に至り春日宮を再称。[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）村社、[[昭和]]12年（[[1937年]]）、県社に列した。 ==祭事・年中行事== *祇園大祭（黒崎祇園山笠）：7月21日～23日 *秋季大祭（例大祭）：10月15日 ==所在地・交通== 福岡県北九州市八幡西区藤田1-10-44 *[[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[黒崎駅]]から徒歩10分 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:県社]] [[Category:八幡西区]]

黒田神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |}} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[八幡西区]][[黒崎 (北九州市)|黒崎]]地区にある[[神社]]で、春日宮や黒田神社とも称される。 『[[日本三代実録]]』に記載される「筑前国鳥野神」に充てられる[[国史見在社]]の[[論社]]もっとも、「筑前国鳥野神」については福岡県[[直方市]]頓野の[[鳥野神社]]に充てる説が有力視されている（『神道大辞典』、平凡社、昭和14年等）。。毎年7月に春日宮へ奉納する[[黒崎祇園山笠]]は北九州の風物詩として有名。 == 祭神 == [[春日四神]]（[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[天児屋根命]]、'''比売神'''）の他、[[福岡藩]]祖である[[黒田孝高|黒田如水]]、および初代藩主・[[黒田長政]]（黒崎大明神）と[[黒田二十四騎]]を併せて祀る。 == 歴史 == 古くは鳥野神社と称し、『日本三代実録』の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]15年（[[873年]]）に神位の等級を進められた「鳥野神」に充てられている。中世は領主・[[麻生氏]]の崇敬篤く、近世に至り[[黒田氏]]が筑前へ入国すると黒崎宿・藤田の鎮守神として栄えた。後に藩祖・黒田如水および初代藩主・黒田長政を祀り、併せて黒田家家中で勲功のあった24人の[[武将]]を顕彰。[[江戸時代]]を通じて「黒田大明神」または長政の神号、「黒崎大明神」を称した。[[明治]]に至り春日宮と再称。同5年（[[1872年]]）[[村社]]、[[昭和]]12年（[[1937年]]）、[[県社]]に列した。 == 祭事 == *祇園大祭（黒崎祇園山笠）：7月21日 - 23日 *秋季大祭（例大祭）：10月15日 == 交通 == *[[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[黒崎駅]]から徒歩10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや きたきゆうしゆうし}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:北九州市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|Shinto shrines in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture|a shrine in Moji-ku of the same name|Kasuga Shrine (Moji-ku, Kitakyushu)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | image = [[File:Torinokasuga.JPG|260px]] | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|55.4|N|130|46|23.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:25000_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|比売神}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga no Yashiro|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * **Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa**: July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * **Autumn Grand Festival**: October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * **By Train**: 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * **By Car**: Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]]

Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * **Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa**: July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * **Autumn Grand Festival**: October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * **By Train**: 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * **By Car**: Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]]

Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga no Yashiro|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * **Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa**: July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * **Autumn Grand Festival**: October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * **By Train**: 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * **By Car**: Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]]

The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga no Yashiro|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * **Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa**: July 21–23. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * **By Train**: 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * **By Car**: Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]]

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga no Yashiro|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * **Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa**: July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * **Autumn Grand Festival**: October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * **By Train**: 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * **By Car**: Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]]

It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga no Yashiro|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]]

The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]]

{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|比売神}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga no Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]]

Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga no Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]]

Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga no Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]]

Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga no Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]][[Category:qq]]

Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, {{otheruses|Shinto shrines in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture|a shrine in Moji-ku of the same name|Kasuga Shrine (Moji-ku, Kitakyushu)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Torinokasuga.JPG|260px]] | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|55.4|N|130|46|23.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:25000_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|比売神}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking {{otheruses|Shinto shrines in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture|a shrine in Moji-ku of the same name|Kasuga Shrine (Moji-ku, Kitakyushu)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Torinokasuga.JPG|260px]] | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|55.4|N|130|46|23.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:25000_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|比売神}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:春日神社 (北九州市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} (disputed location)

Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15th year }}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5th year }} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12nd year }}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5th year }} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12nd year }}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking {{otheruses|Shinto shrines in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture|a shrine in Moji-ku of the same name|Kasuga Shrine (Moji-ku, Kitakyushu)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Torinokasuga.JPG|260px]] | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu|ja|八幡西区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|55.4|N|130|46|23.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:25000_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Shrine}} | deity = * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|12=draft}} ** [[Takemikazuchi]]-o ** [[Futsunushi]] ** [[Ame-no-Koyane]] ** [[Himegami]] ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=draft}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|12=draft}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|ja|黒崎|12=draft}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|ja|八幡西区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市|12=draft}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=draft}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=draft}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami|12=draft}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高|12=draft}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=draft}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15th year|12=draft}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=draft}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏|12=draft}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|12=draft}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5th year|12=draft}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12nd year|12=draft}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅|12=draft}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qq]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]]

It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市|12=draft|12=draft}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=draft|12=draft}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=draft|12=draft}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft|12=draft}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=draft|12=draft}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15th year|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5th year|12=draft|12=draft}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qq]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]]

The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=draft|12=draft}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=draft|12=draft}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft|12=draft}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=draft|12=draft}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15th year|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5th year|12=draft|12=draft}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories|}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qq]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]]
