The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=draft|12=draft}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=draft|12=draft}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|lt=Hime no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|ja|黒田孝高|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft|12=draft}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=draft|12=draft}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15th year|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|ja|黒田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5th year|12=draft|12=draft}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|ja|黒崎駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shrines associated with the Kuroda clan]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qq]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]]

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
22 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:春日神社 (北九州市) up to rev 99964411
revision.text:
{{otheruses|福岡県北九州市八幡西区の神社|同門司区の同名神社|春日神社 (北九州市門司区)}} {{神社 |名称 = 春日神社 |画像 = [[File:Torinokasuga.JPG|260px]] |所在地 = 福岡県北九州市八幡西区藤田1丁目10-44 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|51|55.4|N|130|46|23.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:25000_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = 武甕槌命
経津主命
天児屋根命
比売神
黒崎大明神 |社格 = 国史見在社論社
旧県社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 春日宮
鳥野神社
黒田神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |}} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[八幡西区]][[黒崎 (北九州市)|黒崎]]地区にある[[神社]]で、春日宮や黒田神社とも称される。 『[[日本三代実録]]』に記載される「筑前国鳥野神」に充てられる[[国史見在社]]の[[論社]]もっとも、「筑前国鳥野神」については福岡県[[直方市]]頓野の[[鳥野神社]]に充てる説が有力視されている（『神道大辞典』、平凡社、昭和14年等）。。毎年7月に春日宮へ奉納する[[黒崎祇園山笠]]は北九州の風物詩として有名。 == 祭神 == [[春日四神]]（[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[天児屋根命]]、'''比売神'''）の他、[[福岡藩]]祖である[[黒田孝高|黒田如水]]、および初代藩主・[[黒田長政]]（黒崎大明神）と[[黒田二十四騎]]を併せて祀る。 == 歴史 == 古くは鳥野神社と称し、『日本三代実録』の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]15年（[[873年]]）に神位の等級を進められた「鳥野神」に充てられている。中世は領主・[[麻生氏]]の崇敬篤く、近世に至り[[黒田氏]]が筑前へ入国すると黒崎宿・藤田の鎮守神として栄えた。後に藩祖・黒田如水および初代藩主・黒田長政を祀り、併せて黒田家家中で勲功のあった24人の[[武将]]を顕彰。[[江戸時代]]を通じて「黒田大明神」または長政の神号、「黒崎大明神」を称した。[[明治]]に至り春日宮と再称。同5年（[[1872年]]）[[村社]]、[[昭和]]12年（[[1937年]]）、[[県社]]に列した。 == 祭事 == *祇園大祭（黒崎祇園山笠）：7月21日 - 23日 *秋季大祭（例大祭）：10月15日 == 交通 == *[[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[黒崎駅]]から徒歩10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや きたきゆうしゆうし}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:八幡西区の建築物]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:黒田孝高]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|Shinto shrines in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture|a shrine in Moji-ku of the same name|Kasuga Shrine (Moji-ku, Kitakyushu)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Torinokasuga.JPG|260px]] | location = [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|55.4|N|130|46|23.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:25000_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Shrine}} | deity = * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} ** [[Takemikazuchi]]-o ** [[Futsunushi]] ** [[Ame-no-Koyane]] ** [[Himegami]] ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=}} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Kurosaki|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu]] area of [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=}} mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|qq=}} in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nōgata]]. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1937|qq=}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]]

The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1937|qq=}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:八幡西区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]]

Kuroda Shrine | festivals = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Kurosaki|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu]] area of [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1937|qq=}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:八幡西区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]]

The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:八幡西区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|Shinto shrines in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture|a shrine in Moji-ku of the same name|Kasuga Shrine (Moji-ku, Kitakyushu)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Torinokasuga.JPG|260px]] | location = [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|55.4|N|130|46|23.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:25000_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Shrine}} | deity = * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} ** [[Takemikazuchi]]-o ** [[Futsunushi]] ** [[Ame-no-Koyane]] ** [[Himegami]] ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=}} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Kurosaki|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu]] area of [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=}} mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|qq=}} in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nōgata]]. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1937|qq=}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:八幡西区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:黒田孝高]]

The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]]

Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=}} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Kurosaki|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu]] area of [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=}} mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|qq=}} in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nōgata]]. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1937|qq=}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[en:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}

Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Kurosaki|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu]] area of [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=}} mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|qq=}} in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nōgata]]. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=}} in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1937|qq=}}. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}

It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nōgata]]. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in {{ill|1872|ja||1872年|1=1872|2=ja|3=1872年|lt=Meiji 5|lt_ja=明治5年||qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in {{ill|1937|ja||1937年|1=1937|2=ja|3=1937年|lt=Showa 12|lt_ja=昭和12年||qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1937|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1937年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzai-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}

Kuroda Shrine | festivals = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Kurosaki|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu]] area of [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nōgata]]. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1937|Showa 12]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}

Kuroda Shrine | festivals = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Kurosaki|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu]] area of [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]}} mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nōgata]]. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1937|Showa 12]][[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|Shinto shrines in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture|a shrine in Moji-ku of the same name|Kasuga Shrine (Moji-ku, Kitakyushu)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Torinokasuga.JPG|260px]] | location = [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|55.4|N|130|46|23.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:25000_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Shrine}} | deity = * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} ** [[Takemikazuchi]]-o ** [[Futsunushi]] ** [[Ame-no-Koyane]] ** [[Himegami]] ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a }} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Kurosaki|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu]] area of [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a }} mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nōgata]]. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]] replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1937|Showa 12]] replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:八幡西区の建築物]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:黒田孝高]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|Shinto shrines in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture|a shrine in Moji-ku of the same name|Kasuga Shrine (Moji-ku, Kitakyushu)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Torinokasuga.JPG|260px]] | location = [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|55.4|N|130|46|23.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:25000_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Shrine}} | deity = * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} ** [[Takemikazuchi]]-o ** [[Futsunushi]] ** [[Ame-no-Koyane]] ** [[Himegami]] ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a }} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Kurosaki|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu]] area of [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a }} mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nōgata]]. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]] replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1937|Showa 12]] replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:八幡西区の建築物]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:黒田孝高]]

Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]] replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1937|Showa 12]] replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:春日神社]] into [[Category:Kasuga Shrine]] via bot
revision.text:
{{otheruses|Shinto shrines in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture|a shrine in Moji-ku of the same name|Kasuga Shrine (Moji-ku, Kitakyushu)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Torinokasuga.JPG|260px]] | location = [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|51|55.4|N|130|46|23.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:25000_type:shrine|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Shrine}} | deity = * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} ** [[Takemikazuchi]]-o ** [[Futsunushi]] ** [[Ame-no-Koyane]] ** [[Himegami]] ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a }} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Kurosaki|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu]] area of [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a }} mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nōgata]]. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]] replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1937|Showa 12]] replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:八幡西区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]]

See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]] replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度}} in [[:en:1937|Showa 12]] replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kurosaki Station|Kurosaki Station]] (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] [[sn:asuga Shrine (Kitakyūshū)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Yahatanishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]]

Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Kurosaki|Kurosaki, Kitakyushu]] area of [[:en:Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatanishi-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a }} mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nōgata]]. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * [[:en:Himegami|Hime no Kami]] Additionally, it honors the founding lord of [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]], [[:en:Kuroda Yoshitaka|Kuroda Yoshitaka]] (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]] (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|qq=}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda clan]]. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya.
