Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}} | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|WD=Q1044377}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki|en|Kurosaki|WD=Q1793159|lt=Kurosaki, Kitakyushu}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|en|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|WD=Q1044377|lt=Yahatanishi-ku}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nōgata, Fukuoka|en|Nōgata, Fukuoka|WD=Q861296|lt=Nōgata}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|1=Kurosaki Gion Yamagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678183}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Kami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|en|Kuroda Yoshitaka|WD=Q907019|lt=Kuroda Yoshitaka}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|1=Kuroda Nijūyonki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2027225}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579686}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|WD=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5}} replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=Showa 12}} replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|en|Kurosaki Station|WD=Q871645|lt=Kurosaki Station}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{wikidata link|Q11513659}} [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yahatanishi Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|WD=Q1044377|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki|en|Kurosaki|WD=Q1793159|lt=Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|1=Kurosaki}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|en|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|WD=Q1044377|lt=Yahatanishi-ku|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nōgata, Fukuoka|en|Nōgata, Fukuoka|WD=Q861296|lt=Nōgata|1=Nōgata, Fukuoka}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|1=Kurosaki Gion Yamagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678183}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Kami|1=Himegami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|en|Kuroda Yoshitaka|WD=Q907019|lt=Kuroda Yoshitaka|1=Kuroda Yoshitaka}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|1=Kuroda Nijūyonki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2027225}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579686|1=873}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|WD=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan|1=Kuroda clan}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}} replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=Showa 12|1=1937}} replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|en|Kurosaki Station|WD=Q871645|lt=Kurosaki Station|1=Kurosaki Station}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{wikidata link|Q11513659}} [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yahatanishi Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|1=Kurosaki Gion Yamagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678183}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Kami|1=Himegami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|en|Kuroda Yoshitaka|WD=Q907019|lt=Kuroda Yoshitaka|1=Kuroda Yoshitaka}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|1=Kuroda Nijūyonki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2027225}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579686|1=873}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|WD=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan|1=Kuroda clan}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}} replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=Showa 12|1=1937}} replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|en|Kurosaki Station|WD=Q871645|lt=Kurosaki Station|1=Kurosaki Station}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{wikidata link|Q11513659}} [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yahatanishi Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|en|Kurosaki Station|WD=Q871645|lt=Kurosaki Station|1=Kurosaki Station}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{wikidata link|Q11513659}} [[Category:Buildings in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yahatanishi Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] {{otheruses|Shinto shrines in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture|a shrine in Moji-ku of the same name|Kasuga Shrine (Moji-ku, Kitakyushu)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Torinokasuga.JPG | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|en|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|WD=Q1044377|lt=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.865388888889|N|130.77319444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} ** [[Takemikazuchi]]-o ** [[Futsunushi]] ** [[Ame-no-Koyane]] ** [[Himegami]] ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|WD=Q1044377|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki|en|Kurosaki|WD=Q1793159|lt=Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|1=Kurosaki}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|en|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|WD=Q1044377|lt=Yahatanishi-ku|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nōgata, Fukuoka|en|Nōgata, Fukuoka|WD=Q861296|lt=Nōgata|1=Nōgata, Fukuoka}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|1=Kurosaki Gion Yamagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678183}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Kami|1=Himegami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|en|Kuroda Yoshitaka|WD=Q907019|lt=Kuroda Yoshitaka|1=Kuroda Yoshitaka}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|1=Kuroda Nijūyonki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2027225}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579686|1=873}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|WD=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan|1=Kuroda clan}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}} replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=Showa 12|1=1937}} replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|en|Kurosaki Station|WD=Q871645|lt=Kurosaki Station|1=Kurosaki Station}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{wikidata link|Q11513659}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yahatanishi Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|WD=Q1044377|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki|en|Kurosaki|WD=Q1793159|lt=Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|1=Kurosaki}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|en|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|WD=Q1044377|lt=Yahatanishi-ku|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nōgata, Fukuoka|en|Nōgata, Fukuoka|WD=Q861296|lt=Nōgata|1=Nōgata, Fukuoka}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|1=Kurosaki Gion Yamagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678183}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Kami|1=Himegami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|en|Kuroda Yoshitaka|WD=Q907019|lt=Kuroda Yoshitaka|1=Kuroda Yoshitaka}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|1=Kuroda Nijūyonki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2027225}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579686|1=873}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|WD=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan|1=Kuroda clan}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}} replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=Showa 12|1=1937}} replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|en|Kurosaki Station|WD=Q871645|lt=Kurosaki Station|1=Kurosaki Station}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{wikidata link|Q11513659}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yahatanishi Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] {{otheruses|Shinto shrines in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture|a shrine in Moji-ku of the same name|Kasuga Shrine (Moji-ku, Kitakyushu)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Torinokasuga.JPG | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|en|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|WD=Q1044377|lt=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.865388888889|N|130.77319444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} ** [[Takemikazuchi]]-o ** [[Futsunushi]] ** [[Ame-no-Koyane]] ** [[Himegami]] ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}
Black Warrior Daimyōjin (黒崎大明神) | Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} (disputed location)
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | alternate_names = Kasuga-no-miya
Torino Shrine
Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|WD=Q1044377|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki|en|Kurosaki|WD=Q1793159|lt=Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|1=Kurosaki}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|en|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|WD=Q1044377|lt=Yahatanishi-ku|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nōgata, Fukuoka|en|Nōgata, Fukuoka|WD=Q861296|lt=Nōgata|1=Nōgata, Fukuoka}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|1=Kurosaki Gion Yamagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678183}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Kami|1=Himegami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|en|Kuroda Yoshitaka|WD=Q907019|lt=Kuroda Yoshitaka|1=Kuroda Yoshitaka}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|1=Kuroda Nijūyonki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2027225}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579686|1=873}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|WD=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan|1=Kuroda clan}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}} replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=Showa 12|1=1937}} replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|en|Kurosaki Station|WD=Q871645|lt=Kurosaki Station|1=Kurosaki Station}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{wikidata link|Q11513659}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yahatanishi Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|1=Kurosaki Gion Yamagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678183}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Kami|1=Himegami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|en|Kuroda Yoshitaka|WD=Q907019|lt=Kuroda Yoshitaka|1=Kuroda Yoshitaka}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|1=Kuroda Nijūyonki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2027225}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579686|1=873}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|WD=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan|1=Kuroda clan}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}} replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=Showa 12|1=1937}} replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|en|Kurosaki Station|WD=Q871645|lt=Kurosaki Station|1=Kurosaki Station}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{wikidata link|Q11513659}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yahatanishi Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|qid=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|qid=Q1044377|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki|en|Kurosaki|qid=Q1793159|lt=Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|1=Kurosaki}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|en|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|qid=Q1044377|lt=Yahatanishi-ku|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |1=Shakaku|qid=Q10444029}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nōgata, Fukuoka|en|Nōgata, Fukuoka|qid=Q861296|lt=Nōgata|1=Nōgata, Fukuoka}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|1=Kurosaki Gion Yamagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11678183}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|qid=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Kami|1=Himegami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|qid=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|en|Kuroda Yoshitaka|qid=Q907019|lt=Kuroda Yoshitaka|1=Kuroda Yoshitaka}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|qid=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|1=Kuroda Nijūyonki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2027225}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579686|1=873}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|qid=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan|1=Kuroda clan}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}} replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=Showa 12|1=1937}} replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|en|Kurosaki Station|qid=Q871645|lt=Kurosaki Station|1=Kurosaki Station}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{wikidata link|Q11513659}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yahatanishi Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Kuroda Shrine | festivals = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|qid=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} | location = {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku|qid=Q1044377|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Kurosaki|en|Kurosaki|qid=Q1793159|lt=Kurosaki, Kitakyushu|1=Kurosaki}} area of {{ill|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|en|Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū|qid=Q1044377|lt=Yahatanishi-ku|1=Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyūshū}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is also referred to as Kasuga-no-miya, Torino Shrine, or Kuroda Shrine. It is associated with the {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki s to 社格|comment=enwiki is a |1=Shakaku|qid=Q10444029}} mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, but this attribution is debated.The "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is more commonly associated with {{ill|Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja|鳥野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torino Shrine (Nōgata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nōgata, Fukuoka|en|Nōgata, Fukuoka|qid=Q861296|lt=Nōgata|1=Nōgata, Fukuoka}}. See: ''Shinto Taijiten'', Heibonsha, 1939. The shrine is renowned for the {{ill|Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|ja|黒崎祇園山笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa|1=Kurosaki Gion Yamagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11678183}}, a celebrated summer festival. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 春日神}}: * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}} * [[Ame-no-Koyane]] * {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|qid=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Kami|1=Himegami}} Additionally, it honors the founding lord of {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|qid=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}, {{ill|Kuroda Yoshitaka|en|Kuroda Yoshitaka|qid=Q907019|lt=Kuroda Yoshitaka|1=Kuroda Yoshitaka}} (Kuroda Kanbei), the first feudal lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|qid=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}} (Black Warrior Daimyōjin), and the {{ill|Kuroda 24 Generals|ja|黒田二十四騎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda 24 Generals|1=Kuroda Nijūyonki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2027225}}, a group of notable warriors who served the Kuroda family. == History == The shrine, originally called Torino Shrine, is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, where the "Torino Shrine of Chikuzen Province" is recorded as receiving a Rank promotion in {{ill|873|ja|貞観15年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/873|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579686|1=873}}. During the medieval period, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Aso clan|ja|麻生氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso clan|qq=}}, and in the early modern period, it became a tutelary shrine for Kurosaki and Fujita under the patronage of the {{ill|Kuroda clan|en|Kuroda clan|qid=Q2975305|lt=Kuroda clan|1=Kuroda clan}}. Later, it came to honor Kuroda Yoshitaka, his son Kuroda Nagamasa, and 24 loyal warriors of the Kuroda family. Throughout the Edo period, it was referred to as "Kuroda Daimyōjin" or "Black Warrior Daimyōjin." In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed Kasuga-no-miya. It was designated a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}} replacements]] and promoted to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=Showa 12|1=1937}} replacements]]. == Festivals == * '''Kurosaki Gion Yamakasa''': July 21–23. A famous summer festival where portable shrines (yamakasa) are paraded. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''': October 15. The shrine’s annual major festival. == Access == * '''By Train''': 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kurosaki Station|en|Kurosaki Station|qid=Q871645|lt=Kurosaki Station|1=Kurosaki Station}} (JR Kyushu). * '''By Car''': Accessible via nearby roads and parking facilities. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (北九州市)|version=99964411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市)]] [[ja:春日神社 (北九州市八幡西区)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{wikidata link|Q11513659}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yahatanishi-ku, Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yahatanishi Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Kuroda Yoshitaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
